channel.title: My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content on My New Hugo Site

item.title: Week 3
item.description:
Blog Setup

This week was really challenging in comparison to the previous two. I’m grateful that the assignment was broken up into smaller tasks, making it easier to comprehend.

Task 1

Our first task of the week was to create a GitHub account, which was simple.

Task 2

Next, we were supposed to create a SSH key and add it to our Github account that we had previously created. This SSH key is meant to help write into our GitHub repositories using a private key file. The entire concept of SSH keys are pretty foreign to me; so figuring out how to work with them and to connect it took more time than anticipated as I went in clueless about how it works, or what each line I run means, or, even worse, what each error means. Through many sessions troubleshooting, I finally was able to navigate my way through and connect my SSH key to my GitHub account.

item.title: Week 1
item.description:
Markdown, Zettlr, and Summarising

In the first class, I learnt about wiki and how it works. Although I have been using Wiki for almost my entire life, I wasn’t aware of its power and the social connection it provided. Wiki, the way I view it, is a huge notebook that the whole world can write on. Wiki connects the world by sharing information and correcting misinformation. Although the topic of Wiki itself is not new, the complexity of it was.

item.title: Week 2
item.description:
Reading Summary

Our first task of the week was to install Hugo and Git. One of the most challenging parts was installing the right version of Hugo and then installing Git through Hugo. Thankfully, I was able to look up step-by-step procedures to install Git.

For the rest of the class, me and my group typed out our summarization of the book from last week onto Outline. It was interesting to see last week’s concepts of Markdown being applied here. The notes I took really helped me put together the summarization. We first put down a blurb for the chapters assigned to us, then proceeded to summarize what each chapter says. The next part was really fun as we were to put down what we thought of the readings and what we liked and disliked about it.

item.title: My Post
item.description: Hi! I’m Nithya! This is my first post for my BRIS blog. I hope you enjoyyy! ;)
